fellowship: AA - BIPOC Women & Non-Binary people (See notes)
name: Still I Rise
notes: Still I Rise is an AA meeting for all BIPOC women and non-binary folk and we meet 7 days a week.

name: Morning Meditation
notes: This established sangha provides daily silent meditation space solely designated for BIPOC in recovery. Whether you’re looking for a consistent routine to start your morning, or to level up your existing practice, the 30 minute silent sit is a great way to accomplish that. While not required, participants are invited to sit with cameras turned on to facilitate a community of practice. As such, head wraps, bonnets, do rags are, or whatever your hair care routine consists of, is not only welcomed, but encouraged!

fellowship: All Recovery - African and Afro-Caribbean diaspora, including those of mixed heritage (see notes)
name: Black Recovery Matters (see notes)
notes: All Black Lives in our recovery group Matter, which means that we accept, respect and hold space for Black people of all genders, sexualities and none, faith, spiritualities and none, people with disabilities, neuro-diversity, and illnesses. As a community, we are responsible for holding each other accountable surrounding the awareness of other intersections of oppression such as gender, class, sexuality, disability, etc.

name: RD BIPOC: Under the Bodhi Tree
notes:
A compassionate program with a foundation from Buddhist Principles. 75-minute meeting open to individuals who identify as BIPOC. We meditate together & read from the Recovery Dharma book. There's also space for personal shares.
3rd Sunday: Speaker Meeting

name: BIPOC Two Way Prayer
notes: This is a Tenth, Eleventh, and Twelve Step Guidance Group focused on the practice of Two Way Prayer. We welcome any BIPOC member of any 12 Step fellowship, regardless of religion, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, or any other reason.

fellowship: AA - Women, Non-Binary, Two-Spirit, Trans and Intersex
name: Recovery is Colorful
BIPOC Shining & Recieving
notes: This meeting is only open to Black, Indigenous, People of Color in UA. We do this to safely explore all factors of our underearning including race. The focus of this meeting is about our repetitive patterns of hiding and biting as well as the gifts of recovery in UA - which are shining and receiving.

name: Stoners of Color
notes: Stoners of color is a fiercely LGBTQ+ inclusive Marijuana Anonymous group specifically for people who identify as Black, Indigenous, or other People of color. This meeting has a varying format between topics, speakers, steps, traditions, and stories

fellowship: All Recovery - Atheists, Agnostics and Freethinkers
name: BIPOC Freethinkers
Sunday Gay Men's Rap
notes: The format varies week by week and rotates between speaker discussion, round robin, and step meeting. We are 99% BIPOC but the meeting is open to everyone so it isn't exclusively a BIPOC meeting. Meeting Room opens a half hour early for fellowship.

fellowship: Sex and Love Addicts Anonymous (SLAA) - (see notes)
name: Spiritual Diversity
notes: BIPOC ONLY "As Black, Indigenous and People of Color, our sex and love addiction has often been impacted by our current experiences or historical legacies as targets of racism, discrimination and prejudice... Throughout history, racism has often included sexual domination as part of white supremacy. This includes the sexual abuse and sexual harassment of people of color by white people, as well as training people of color to think of themselves as animals, as someone else's property, as only good for work, and expendable...Many of us come to SLAA because we have a problem, and we find community. It is a wonderful relief to know that we are not alone with our sex and love addiction. However, for some people of color, it may be difficult to be in a community that is predominantly white." -From "People of Color in SLAA"

name: Women's 11th Step Meditation
notes: This meeting is for black, indigenous and womxn of color, and their womxn allies.

name: What's the T?
notes: WHAT'S THE T? is a midday check-in meeting designated for members of the CMA community who identify as Black, Indigenous People Of Color (BIPOC). This gathering provides a confidential, supportive space for BIPOC individuals to decompress and speak openly about recovery-related trauma in a safe and affirming environment.

name: Spiritual Journey
notes: 1 Hour meeting - For more info visit: https://www.cmainla.com/meetings/spiritual-journey/?tsml-day=0 or www.cmainla.com

name: OA Ebony and POC BB Study
Soul Sundays
notes: This meeting is only for POC and those in recovery, we are a listed Al Anon meeting.

fellowship: Sex and Love Addicts Anonymous (SLAA) (other fellowships welcome, using "Gentle Path Through the 12-Steps" Book) (see notes)
name: SLAA POC Step Writing Meeting
notes: This meeting is open to any BIPOC working their recovery in the "Gentle Path Through the 12-Steps" book. Other fellowships are welcome!

fellowship: Survivors of Incest Anonymous (SIA) - Men (see notes)
name: Male Survivors of Color
notes: This is a new meeting for male survivors of childhood sexual abuse under the fellowship of Survivors of Incest Anonymous. This meeting is for men of color who were sexually abused as children by family members or trusted members of a community,

name: BIPOC of EA
notes:
"Click here, to access the BIPOC of EA meeting Google Doc.  Emotions Anonymous is for anyone who wants to become well emotionally.  BIPOC of EA (Emotions Anonymous) is a safe, sacred meeting space ONLY for people who identify as Black (African American), Indigenous (Native American), and People of Color.  People of Color refers to any non-White identity, including Latinx/Hispanic, Asian, Pacific Islander, Middle Eastern, etc.  Some may prefer to identify as members of the Global Majority.  In this sacred space, we can share without fear of retaliation or judgment.  

If you do NOT identify as BIPOC or members of the Global Majority, the best way that you can support us is by not attending the meeting and honoring our sacred space.

name: Women+ Meeting
notes: This meeting is for anyone who is Black and identifies as girl, woman, femme, or nonbinary. www.soberblackgirlsclub.org

fellowship: Sexual Compulsive Anonymous - People of Color
name: SCA - People of Color

fellowship: Sex and Love Addicts Anonymous (SLAA) (see notes)
name: SLAA POC
notes:
This is a closed meeting for self-identified People of Color (Black, African, Native, Indigenous, Latinx, Caribbean, Arab, Asian, and Pacific Islander, people of color from the Middle East, or anyone of mixed racial heritage). The purpose of this meeting is to share the intersectional experiences of being a sexually compulsive person and being a person
of color and recovering in this program. "As Black, Indigenous and People of Color, our sex and love addiction has often been impacted by our current experiences or historical legacies as targets of racism, discrimination and prejudice... Throughout history, racism has often included sexual domination as part of white supremacy. This includes the sexual abuse and sexual harassment of people of color by white people, as well as training people of color to think of themselves as animals, as someone else's property, as only good for work, and expendable...Many of us come to SLAA because we have a problem, and we find community. It is a wonderful relief to know that we are not alone with our sex and love addiction. However, for some people of color, it may be difficult to be in a community that is predominantly white." -From "People of Color in SLAA".

name: African American Blue Book Study (camera on)
notes: This is a CLOSED Co-Dependents Anonymous Blue Book Study specifically for foundational (Black) African Americans. Please respect the focus of this group. This is an online (zoom) meeting. Please note, the group must be able to see you on video in real time. This means your video camera must be on at all times. Please do not be offended, but the meeting will not be able to accommodate anyone who does not have working video or does not wish to be seen on camera.

fellowship: Adult Children of Alcoholics/Dysfunctional Families (ACA) (see notes)
name: ACA MASALA
notes: This gathering is intended for Adult Children of South Asian descent who live or have lived outside South Asia. We welcome people of all identities and forms of expression. These include but are not limited to race, ethnicity, nationality, caste, gender, sexual orientation, age, disability, religion, relationship, occupational, health, or socioeconomic status.The MASALA acronym roughly approximates to Multicultural Adult Children of South Asian Ancestry Loving Association/Living in America/Abroad. For safety reasons, we ask that newcomers introduce themselves briefly with video on at their first meeting.

name: Overaters Anonymous API
notes: Asian and Pacific Islanders is the Special Focus of this meeting. Our intent of this meeting is to hear recovery from people who identify as Asian, Pacific Islanders. We prioritize sharing for those who identify as Asian, Pacific Islanders.

fellowship: Debtors Anonymous and Business Debtors Anonymous (see notes)
name: The Color of Clarity
notes: BDA format on odd-numbered months, DA format on even-numbered months

fellowship: Sex and Love Addicts Anonymous (SLAA) - Men Color who are gay, bisexual, questioning or other gender identified (see notes)
name: 12 Step/12 Tradition SLAA for Gay Men of Color
notes: By group conscience our group is open only to Gay Men of Color who are gay, bisexual, questioning or other gender identified. In our meeting, we support the exploration of the connections between racism, homophobia and our SLAA addictive and/or anorexic behaviors. For our purposes we define a Gay Man of Color as of: African descent; Native American heritage; Latino; Chicano; Asian/Pacific islander; or Man of Color from the Middle East; or anyone of mixed racial heritage including one or more of the above.

name: BlackStar: Sober and Black
notes: BlackStar is an open Alcoholics Anonymous meeting for Black people, including those within the African diaspora

fellowship: Adult Children of Alcoholics/Dysfunctional Families (ACA) (Closed* All Gender)
name: Black in ACA
notes: *Closed to those who identify as Black or part of the African diaspora

fellowship: AA - BIPOC Women & genderqueer people (See notes)
name: Spiritual Deep Conditioner
notes: Spiritual Deep Conditioner (SDC) is an AA meeting for anyone identifying as a woman, and/or transgender, nonbinary, two-spirit, intersex, or other gender-expansive identities, who is also a Person Of Color.

name: Asian Pacific Islander Diaspora Al-Anon Family Group
notes: Open to all who self identify as Asian, Asian American, Pacific Islander, South Asian, South West Asian, Middle Eastern, and Native Hawaiian. We also welcome those of biracial or multiracial heritage who identify within the API community.

name: Family Meal
notes: BIPOC ONLY - Everyone in attendance needs to adhere to guidelines that are emailed out in advance to obtaining the password. Guidelines are "refrain from any cross talk, bullying, hate speech, fragility, homophobic or racist comments. We ask that you respect the speaker. The intention of this meeting is to uplift and support Black, Indigenous, and People of Color (BIPOC) and LGBTQIA BIOPC in recovery.

fellowship: Asians, Asian Americans, Pacific Islanders, and South Asians ACA meeting (See notes)
name: Safe Spaces for Asians, Asian Americans, Pacific Islanders, and South Asians
notes: We will read from "Strengthening My Recovery" and then personal shares. Only Asians, Asian Americans, Pacific Islanders, and South Asians welcome. This meeting is 1 hour. This meeting meets, Sunday 7pm, Tuesday 8 pm, Wednesday 8pm, and Friday at 6:30 pm Pacific Times.

name: BIPOC Sunday evening Al-Anon Group
notes: The meeting is BIPOC only, but is a disability justice, all-immigration-statuses welcoming, and trans & queer friendly space. This is a closed, unregistered, Black, Indigenous, People of Color meeting. We do this to explore all factors of our recovery, including healing from colonization and racism. Our meeting is closed for the safety of those in the meeting. // Do not be alarmed if nobody is on the zoom in the first 10 minutes or so. This meeting may start late, but it is running.

fellowship: AA - BIPOC Women & Non-Binary people (See Notes)
name: Still I Rise
notes: Still I Rise is an AA meeting for all BIPOC women and non-binary folk and we meet 7 days a week.

name: Morning Meditation
notes: This established sangha provides daily silent meditation space solely designated for BIPOC in recovery. Whether you’re looking for a consistent routine to start your morning, or to level up your existing practice, the 30 minute silent sit is a great way to accomplish that. While not required, participants are invited to sit with cameras turned on to facilitate a community of practice. As such, head wraps, bonnets, do rags are, or whatever your hair care routine consists of, is not only welcomed, but encouraged!

name: National BIPOC Recovery Meeting
notes: This meeting is 1.5hrs long. 1st & 3rd Monday of the month (subject to change). Recovery is a personal journey for each individual! This support group is dedicated to people of color who have opted for abstinence from problematic substances or behaviors. Remember that recovery also encompasses detoxing from emotions like anger, fear, resentment, and trauma. This space is for you! This National BIPOC virtual meeting is designed to provide young adults of color with a safe and intentional space to learn and explore SMART's tools, feel a sense of belonging, and embrace a supportive community. The tools and conversations will enable you to feel empowered and take control of your thoughts and behaviors. Facilitators: Markita and Jas

name: BIPOC Parents in Recovery
zoomLink: Please Register first at the Virtual Meeting Link https://us02web.zoom.us/meeting/register/tZcldeCvqz8jHdC2OJSkaPx20b8RPSoU_OHQ  After registering ONCE, use the link emailed to you.
notes: This is an ACA meeting focused on parenting while re-parenting to “Stop the generational transmission of dysfunction.” // Child-care friendly. People of Color (POC) or People of the Global Majority (PGM) includes anyone who identifies as African, Asian, indigenous Australian, indigenous South, Central and North American, people of color from the Islands and the Middle East or anyone of mixed racial heritage including one or more of the above.

name: BIPOC SLAA/CODA
notes: A closed cross addiction meeting for those who identify as BIPOC/PGM and as sex and love addicts and codependents or think they have a problem with one or both.

name: Black Sheep
notes: While we adhere to the third tradition, 'the only requirement for membership is a desire to stop drinking' we ask that all non-BIPOC attendees please refrain from sharing.

fellowship: All Recovery - Womxn, Non-Binary, Two-Spirit and Trans
name: Kin In Recovery
Three Factors
notes: 1st Mondays and 3rd Thursdays. Format is Discussion, Speaker, Workshop. We welcome PGM/BIPOC persons who have struggled with existential life crises where what is isn't what ought to be. We explore three factors of personality development to support transformation. If you grew up with intensities in a dysfunctional environment, help us create a neurodivergent-affirming ACA community. Listed on Greater East Bay ACA Intergroup

name: Detaching From White Supremacy
notes: BIPOC ONLY - We are here to detach from ALL forms of ( and we do not collude with) White Supremacy. Aware of the insidious, invidious pervasive forms of White supremacy including institutional & systemic racism, white fragility, and implict bias and we labor to liberate ourselfves one day at a time as we focus on Healing Trauma, Abuse, our Inner Child & Meditation.

fellowship: Debtors Anonymous - Womxn, Non-Binary, Two-Spirit, Trans and Intersex
name: WOC Visions
UNITY - BIPOC & Allies in Recovery
notes:
15 minute lead share followed by 3 min open shares from the community

🙏🏾📖 🔥🎆🙏🏼✨

A safe space for members of the Black, Indigenous, People of Color and LGBTQ community to recover from ALL addictions.

*While this meeting centers the recovery of BIPOC/LGBTQ folks, all are welcome to participate!*

name: Women of Color AFG (Al-Anon)
notes: Our meeting is Literature focused, and we study the Steps, Traditions and Concepts in Al-Anon. We welcome all, with a special focus on women-identifying members of the global majority.

fellowship:
OA - BIPOC and Allies Tool Meeting (lifeline literature/ meditation/ writing) Newcomers welcome!
(see notes)
name: BIPOC Lifeline
notes: This is a tools oriented meeting with time for literature, meditation, writing, and action plans.

name: POC  Recovery 11th Step Prayer & Meditation Reflection Meeting
notes: For Bi-POC Only, meeting is from 7:30-8:45pm

fellowship: Black Women’s Special Interest AA meeting
name: Mocha Mondays

fellowship: AA - BIPOC Women & Non-Binary people (See Notes)
name: Still I Rise
notes: Still I Rise is an AA meeting for all BIPOC women and non-binary folk and we meet 7 days a week.

name: Morning Meditation
notes: This established sangha provides daily silent meditation space solely designated for BIPOC in recovery. Whether you’re looking for a consistent routine to start your morning, or to level up your existing practice, the 30 minute silent sit is a great way to accomplish that. While not required, participants are invited to sit with cameras turned on to facilitate a community of practice. As such, head wraps, bonnets, do rags are, or whatever your hair care routine consists of, is not only welcomed, but encouraged!

name: RD BIPOC Tuesday
notes: A compassionate program with a foundation from Buddhist Principles. 60-minute meeting open to individuals who identify as BIPOC.

fellowship: Sober Black Girls Club (First and Third Tuesday of the month)
name: Queer and Sober
notes: This meeting is on the 1st and 3rd Tuesday of the month and is for anyone who is Black, Brown, Indigenous, or identifies with a community of color and queer (LGBTQIA+). www.soberblackgirlsclub.org

name: BIPOC and Al-Anon Principles: Detaching From White Supremacy

fellowship: Adult Children of Alcoholics/Dysfunctional Families (ACA) (see notes)
name: BIPOC Rainbow ACA
notes: Reminder: This is a video-on meeting. Also, this is a closed ACA Group for Black, Indigenous + People of Colour who are lesbian, gay, bisexual, trans, intersex, queer, asexual and those questioning their gender + sexuality. Meeting is locked after about 20 mins.

name: Survivors of Color
notes: The password for this group is changed every three months. You can email the point of contact to get on their email list to receive the password update. They also can provide an updated list of other Survivors of Incest Anonymous meetings.

name: BIPOC CoDA
notes: A closed  meeting for those who identify as BIPOC/PGM and as codependents or think they have a problem with codependency.

name: DMV BIPOC ACA
notes: Big Red Book meeting, 60 min

fellowship: Sex and Love Addicts Anonymous - Women, Non-Binary, Two-Spirit, Trans and Intersex
name: You Are Not Alone
notes: "As Black, Indigenous and People of Color, our sex and love addiction has often been impacted by our current experiences or historical legacies as targets of racism, discrimination and prejudice... Throughout history, racism has often included sexual domination as part of white supremacy. This includes the sexual abuse and sexual harassment of people of color by white people, as well as training people of color to think of themselves as animals, as someone else's property, as only good for work, and expendable...Many of us come to SLAA because we have a problem, and we find community. It is a wonderful relief to know that we are not alone with our sex and love addiction. However, for some people of color, it may be difficult to be in a community that is predominantly white." -From "People of Color in SLAA"

name: Technicolor Road to Happy Destiny
notes: This all inclusive meeting welcomes BIPOC, LGBTQIA+ persons, all allies, all ages, all genders, all ethnicities, and all religious expressions. We encourage you to share your pronouns with us when you introduce yourself, if you so wish. Attendees are free to discuss any topics that affect their sobriety. We do not tolerate racism, hate speech, or sexually aggressive language. As a group we are responsible for calling it out whenever we hear or see it to ensure the safety of the meeting for everyone.

fellowship: Debtors Anonymous - African-American and African Diaspora
name: Lift Every Debt and Earn
Just for Tuesday BIPOC SLAA
notes: closed meeting - only those identifying with sex and love addiction may attend

name: Mixed Emotions
notes: A closed AA meeting for People of Color Only

name: Indigenous Recovery
notes: This meeting is for those in recovery who identify as Indigenous. One hour meeting

fellowship: All Recovery - All self-identifying women and non-binary people of color - Literature/Discussion 90min meeting
name: Colorful Meeting
notes: All Recovery - All self-identifying women and non-binary people of color - Literature/Discussion 90min meeting

fellowship: Asians, Asian Americans, Pacific Islanders, and South Asians ACA meeting (See notes)
name: Safe Spaces for Asians, Asian Americans, Pacific Islanders, and South Asians
notes: We will read from "Strengthening My Recovery" and then personal shares. Only Asians, Asian Americans, Pacific Islanders, and South Asians welcome. This meeting is 1 hour. This meeting meets, Sunday 7pm, Tuesday 8 pm, Wednesday 8pm, and Friday at 6:30 pm Pacific Times.

fellowship: AA - BIPOC Women & Non-Binary people (See Notes)
name: Still I Rise
notes: Still I Rise is an AA meeting for all BIPOC women and non-binary folk and we meet 7 days a week.

name: BIPOC Life Ring "How was your week"
contact: For more info check out: https://lifering.org/online-meetings/
notes: LifeRing Secular Recovery is an organization of people who share practical experiences and sobriety support. There are as many ways to live free of illicit or non-medically indicated drugs and alcohol as there are stories of successful sober people. Many LifeRing members attend other kinds of meetings or recovery programs, and we honor those decisions. Some have had negative experiences in attempting to find help elsewhere, but most people soon find that LifeRing’s emphasis on the positive, practical present-day can turn anger and despair into hope and resolve. LifeRing respectfully embraces what works for each individual.

name: Morning Meditation
notes: This established sangha provides daily silent meditation space solely designated for BIPOC in recovery. Whether you’re looking for a consistent routine to start your morning, or to level up your existing practice, the 30 minute silent sit is a great way to accomplish that. While not required, participants are invited to sit with cameras turned on to facilitate a community of practice. As such, head wraps, bonnets, do rags are, or whatever your hair care routine consists of, is not only welcomed, but encouraged!

fellowship: CoDA - Power of 5 (Po5), 30 Questions (Black Women) - See notes
name: Po5 Black Women of Coda
notes: This meeting is 1.5hrs. Must have attended at least 6 CoDA meetings (online, in-person, phone, etc). Will be rotating the 30 Questions for Steps 1-3. Cameras must be on for the first 15 minutes of the meeting. There will be a 7-minute bio-break at 11:40 am.

name: Black in UA
notes: Meeting for people identifying as Black and or African descent

name: Sober Sistas AA Meeting
notes: A closed AA meeting for Black and mixed-race women of African descent.

fellowship: SLAA - BIPOC Male identifying or non-conforming
name: SLAA BIPOC Men's Midweek Midday 12x12x12 Book Study
notes: A weekly journey through the soon to be released S.L.A.A. 12 Steps, 12 Traditions, & 12 Concepts book "A Foundation For Living", meditation, then sharing. *Closed to BIPOC Male identifying or non-conforming who have a desire to have sanity & sobriety regarding love & sex.

name: BIPOC CoDA
notes: People of the global majority are negatively impacted by institutions and structures of colonialism. This meeting will center the experience of marginalized groups in recovery from systemic codependency. All who desire to healthy loving relationships are welcome to attend. Conference approved literature an non conference approved race recovery literature will be available at this meeting. Please contact wowcafetheater@gmail.com for more info. Type Bipoc Coda in subject line.

fellowship: Adult Children of Alcoholics/Dysfunctional Families (ACA) - BIPOC (see notes)
name: In Living Color
notes: Anyone is welcome as long as they are BIPOC

name: Let It Begin with Me AFG of African Descent (see notes)
notes: This meeting is for people who self-identify as a person of African descent. Please note that by group conscience, video must be on for a fellow to read/share/speak during the meeting.

name: FIRST BIPOC ACA Loving Parent Guidebook (LPG).
notes: This meeting is for veteran and new ACA fellows who want to learn how to live in the solution and become your own loving parent.  Prior knowledge of the YELLOW WORKBOOK or the BIG RED BOOK is helpful.

name: Recovery In Color
notes: An open meeting for all BIPOC that addresses all addictions by studying the book Our recovery In Color: The Twelve Steps For All People Of Color (https://recoveryincolor.com/book/).

name: Rainbow Room
notes: The 8pm EST Wednesday Rainbow Room AA meeting is closed to BIPOC and LGBTQIA people. The speaker speaks for 25 minutes on any topic of their choice.

name: OA Mixed-Race People
notes: New special focus meeting, centering the Experience of Mixed-Race people. We've intentionally created this meeting during a time on Wednesdays when there are MANY other zoom OA meetings happening alongside this one. We ask our white fellows to be good allies by respecting our need for a separate space where we can speak honestly about out experiences in the service of our recovery. All multiracial people, regardless of appearance, as well as BIPOC folx are encouraged to come! We are an LGBTQIA+ welcoming meeting.

name: Stoners of Color
notes: Stoners of color is a fiercely LGBTQ+ inclusive Marijuana Anonymous group specifically for people who identify as Black, Indigenous, or other People of color. This meeting has a varying format between topics, speakers, steps, traditions, and stories

name: QTBIPOC SLAA
notes: Open to those who identify both as Black, Indigenous, or other People of Color and on the LGBTQIA+ spectrum

fellowship: Underearners Anonymous - African-American and African Diaspora
name: Black in UA
contact: Contact email for Zoom ID and password - abundantprosperity@gmail.com
notes: The Black in UA meeting of Underearners Anonymous is an inclusive and safe community for those identifying as Black who are Underearners. By sharing our experience, strength, and hope, and by carrying the message to those who still suffer, we find joy, clarity, and serenity as we recover together.

fellowship: Sex and Love Addicts Anonymous (see notes)
name: SLAA POC
notes: "As Black, Indigenous and People of Color, our sex and love addiction has often been impacted by our current experiences or historical legacies as targets of racism, discrimination and prejudice... Throughout history, racism has often included sexual domination as part of white supremacy. This includes the sexual abuse and sexual harassment of people of color by white people, as well as training people of color to think of themselves as animals, as someone else's property, as only good for work, and expendable...Many of us come to SLAA because we have a problem, and we find community. name: Fifty Shades of Brown
notes: A meeting para gente Latinx. A safe space for queers. Spanglish y travesuras

name: Bridge to Hope (International Chinese and English AA Meeting)
First People’s ACA
notes: 6:30 pm Australian EST This is a closed meeting of ACA for those who identify as Indigenous, First Nations and First Peoples eg Aboriginal, Torres Strait Islander, Māori, Pasifika, Native American, First Nations. We meet to heal from our journeys of alcoholism or other family dysfunction, and from colonisation and racism. We are a Rainbow/Queer friendly meeting. If you do not come from this community, we respectfully encourage you to explore other ACA meetings at www.adultchildren.org

fellowship: AA - BIPOC Women & Non-Binary people (See Notes)
name: Still I Rise
notes: Still I Rise is an AA meeting for all BIPOC women and non-binary folk and we meet 7 days a week.

name: Morning Meditation
notes: This established sangha provides daily silent meditation space solely designated for BIPOC in recovery. Whether you’re looking for a consistent routine to start your morning, or to level up your existing practice, the 30 minute silent sit is a great way to accomplish that. While not required, participants are invited to sit with cameras turned on to facilitate a community of practice. As such, head wraps, bonnets, do rags are, or whatever your hair care routine consists of, is not only welcomed, but encouraged!

name: Three Factors
notes: 1st Mondays and 3rd Thursdays. Format is Discussion, Speaker, Workshop. We welcome PGM/BIPOC persons who have struggled with existential life crises where what is isn't what ought to be. We explore three factors of personality development to support transformation. If you grew up with intensities in a dysfunctional environment, help us create a neurodivergent-affirming ACA community. Listed on Greater East Bay ACA Intergroup

name: People of the Global Majority (PGM) Educators Detaching from the Myth of White Supremacy by Applying Al-Anon Principles.
notes: This NOT an Al-Anon Family Group. We are a group of educators detaching from the fiction of white supremacy by using Al-Anon principles in our educational settings.

fellowship: ACA - Native American and Indigenous People of North America (closed meeting--see notes)
name: First Peoples of Turtle Island People of Color ACA Meeting
notes: This is a closed meeting for People of Color whose ancestry originates from Turtle Island, otherwise known as North America and defined here as the U.S., Canada and Mexico. This includes but is not limited to Native Hawaiian, Native Alaskan, First Nations, and those from federally recognized, state recognized and unrecognized tribal nations, and anyone of mixed Indigenous racial heritage.

name: Queer RD BIPOC
notes: This is a space for members who identify as LGBTQIA2S+ Black, Indigenous, or People of Color. This is a 75-minute meeting that seeks to center transgender, non-binary, & gender expansive voices. Our goal is to provide a safer and braver space for compassionate sharing and deep listening without judgment. RD believes anyone can heal from their suffering and recover; the journey can start now for each of us. This is a program of empowerment to change and heal. We are peer-led and we put our trust in the three jewels of Buddhism.

fellowship: Sex and Love Addicts Anonymous - Black Women (see notes)
name: Black Women in SLAA
notes: "As Black, Indigenous and People of Color, our sex and love addiction has often been impacted by our current experiences or historical legacies as targets of racism, discrimination and prejudice... Throughout history, racism has often included sexual domination as part of white supremacy. This includes the sexual abuse and sexual harassment of people of color by white people, as well as training people of color to think of themselves as animals, as someone else's property, as only good for work, and expendable...Many of us come to SLAA because we have a problem, and we find community. It is a wonderful relief to know that we are not alone with our sex and love addiction. However, for some people of color, it may be difficult to be in a community that is predominantly white." -From "People of Color in SLAA"

fellowship: Adult Children of Alcoholics/Dysfunctional Families (ACA) (see notes)
name: Loving Parent Guidebook (BIPOC Only)
notes: *We will read and explore the new Loving Parent Guidebook (LPG). (Shared on screen via Kindle) This will be a closed BIPOC ACA meeting. The LPG is currently under ACA Fellowship Review. This is an opportunity to survey the LPG from a BIPOC perspective, and contribute to ensuring that ACA literature is culturally inclusive and relevant.

name: Sisters We Choose
zoomLink: Register to receive the zoom info: https://us02web.zoom.us/meeting/register/tZAqcO2vqz8sG9MnlmiwGUsbV5_sB9Z5uHP9#/registration
notes: This meeting is for anyone who is Black and identifies as girl, woman, femme, or nonbinary. www.soberblackgirlsclub.org

fellowship: Adult Children of Alcoholics/Dysfunctional Families (ACA) (see notes)
name: Lovingly Reparenting our Inner Children
notes: Notes: This meeting is open to all People of Color who identify within or outside a marginalized gender. This includes Non-Binary individuals, Transgender Women and Men, souls identifying as Two-Spirit and cisgender Women who are focused on recovery by practicing the ACA Solution which is to become our own Loving Parent. This is a safe space to share your adult, childhood, and racial experiences without being judged.Therefore, if you identify as a cisgender Man and or as White we ask that you practice allyship by finding an alternative meeting. Please note we lock our meeting after the final closed group statement is read, that is roughly within 10 minutes. However, this is a rough estimate so please plan accordingly.

name: Black in Recovery
notes: Formerly the Avenue. Closed AA Speaker Meeting. For those with a desire to stop drinking who identify as Black. 30 minute speaker and then open discussion

name: We Do Recover
notes: In person BIPOC meeting. Los Angles, 2900 Sunset Blvd, Los Angeles, CA 90026

fellowship: Sex and Love Addicts Anonymous - Women of Color (see notes)
name: Thursday Night Healing Journey: Women of Color
notes: This meeting is for Black Indigenous People of Color, Womxn-Identifying, Trans and Non-Binary. Come join us for meditation reading and shares💐

fellowship: AA - BIPOC Women & Non-Binary people (See Notes)
name: Still I Rise
notes: Still I Rise is an AA meeting for all BIPOC women and non-binary folk and we meet 7 days a week.

fellowship: Adult Children of Alcoholics/Dysfunctional Families (ACA) (see notes)
name: BIPOC Rainbow ACA
notes: Reminder: This is a video-on meeting. Also, this is a closed ACA Group for Black, Indigenous + People of Colour who are lesbian, gay, bisexual, trans, intersex, queer, asexual and those questioning their gender + sexuality. Meeting is locked after about 20 mins.

name: Morning Meditation
notes: This established sangha provides daily silent meditation space solely designated for BIPOC in recovery. Whether you’re looking for a consistent routine to start your morning, or to level up your existing practice, the 30 minute silent sit is a great way to accomplish that. While not required, participants are invited to sit with cameras turned on to facilitate a community of practice. As such, head wraps, bonnets, do rags are, or whatever your hair care routine consists of, is not only welcomed, but encouraged!

fellowship: BIPOC All Fellowships - Recovery Work Co-working Space ** Not a meeting (See Notes)
name: Recovery Work Co-Working
notes: This is not a meeting. This is a quiet coworking space for recovery work (step work, journaling, amends, etc), for bipoc people in recovery. We open with the Serenity Prayer. Everyone is welcome to state what they will be working on, verbally or in the chat. 1 minute check-ins at the end of the hour are welcomed but optional. Stay as long or as little as you want 💕

fellowship: BIPOC SPAA - Sex and Porn Addicts Anonymous (See notes)
name: SPAA BIPOC
zoomLink: For login information, reach out through our website, spaa-recovery.org
contact: For login information, reach out through our website, spaa-recovery.org
notes: For login information, reach out through our website, spaa-recovery.org. This meeting is for sex and porn addicts who are people of color, but all are welcome.

name: Black in DA Workshop
notes: For anyone that at identifies as black and or a member of the African Diaspora. It’s a meeting welcome to beginners in DA and it is 1.5hrs in length.

name: Compulsive Spenders Diversity (mixed meeting i.e BIPOC and non-BIPOC)
notes: 75 minute meeting. Money recovery is about uncovering our relationship with ourselves and the society and cultures we live in. We have a social structure that induces trauma in us & thus an addictive desire to escape. Therefore, issues such as racism, classism, homophobia, sexism, gender, internalised-isms, and body issues are not outside issues but fundamentally need to be addressed in a safe space if we're to recover. Sign in to your Zoom account is required.

name: OA Ebony and POC BB Study
Let it Begin with Us - Women of the Global Majority AFG
notes: An Al-Anon meeting for friends and family of alcoholics. This meeting is specifically for those who self-identify as women of the global majority. The meeting format alternates weekly between speaker meetings and study of conference-approved literature.

name: Happy Joyous and Free People of Color AFG
notes: ** Please note this meeting does not begin until August 5, 2022 - Happy Joyous and Free People of Color AFG is an in-to-me-see meeting. We are a book study and have speakers sharing some of themselves.

name: Stoners of Color Grab Bag Meeting
notes: Stoners of color is a fiercely LGBTQ+ inclusive Marijuana Anonymous group specifically for people who identify as Black, Indigenous, or other People of color. This meeting is a grab bag format where we ask questions about our recovery and it lasts 75 minutes.

name: Clutterers of Color
notes: Clutterers of Color is the first BIPOC meeting of Clutterers Anonymous (CLA). Clutter is anything we don’t need, want, or use that takes our time, energy or space, and destroys our serenity. Clutterers of Color was formed as a supportive space for BIPOC.

name: CoDA for Alcoholics & Addicts
contact: Tonya B. Ph: 909-782-9969 or E: allrecoverymeditationmeeting@gmail.com
notes: This meeting is for black, indigenous and womxn of color, and their womxn allies.

name: Cultural Connections: Discerning Independence
location: Hybrid - Calvary Church of Naperville (Address: 9s200 Route 59, Naperville, Illinois 60564) - Door 24
notes: We are a group of people who share a desire to stop drinking. This group is aimed towards people of color seeking recovery; allies are greatly appreciated and encouraged to attend as well in support. This is a closed meeting; therefore, we ask that only those desiring to stop drinking attend this meeting. We meet weekly!

name: The Sleepover BIPOC in AA
notes: Speaker/Discussion 90min meeting

name: Black in Recovery Big Book Study

notes: We will read from "Strengthening My Recovery" and then personal shares. Only Asians, Asian Americans, Pacific Islanders, and South Asians welcome. This meeting is 1 hour. This meeting meets, Sunday 7pm, Tuesday 8 pm, Wednesday 8pm, and Friday at 6:30 pm Pacific Times.

fellowship: Wellbriety Two-Spirit, LGBTQAI+ and allies in recovery on the Red Road (90 min meeting)
name: Two-Spirit Nation Wellbriety
notes: 90 Minute meeting

fellowship: SLAA POC (2nd and 4th Saturday of the month)
name: SLAA POC
notes: Sat 10am GMT, 2nd and 4th Saturday of the month

fellowship: AA - BIPOC Women & Non-Binary people (See Notes)
name: Still I Rise
notes: Still I Rise is an AA meeting for all BIPOC women and non-binary folk and we meet 7 days a week.

name: Morning Meditation
notes: This established sangha provides daily silent meditation space solely designated for BIPOC in recovery. Whether you’re looking for a consistent routine to start your morning, or to level up your existing practice, the 30 minute silent sit is a great way to accomplish that. While not required, participants are invited to sit with cameras turned on to facilitate a community of practice. As such, head wraps, bonnets, do rags are, or whatever your hair care routine consists of, is not only welcomed, but encouraged!

fellowship: People of the Global Majority ACA (see notes)
name: BIPOC ACA Schematic
notes: Per our group conscience, this is a closed meeting for those that self identify as a person of the global majority (aka BIPOC). If you do not identify as a person of the global majority or PGM, we respectfully ask that you practice ally-ship by leaving this meeting. This is a safe space to explore the effects of generational racial trauma.

name: Recovery In Color
notes: An open meeting for all BIPOC that addresses all addictions by studying the book Our recovery In Color: The Twelve Steps For All People Of Color (https://recoveryincolor.com/book/).

name: Recovery Dharma BIPOC: Women and Non-Binary
notes: A 75-minute meditation-based meeting for all Women, Non-Binary, and Gender-expansive people who identify as BIPOC (Black, Indigenous, and People of Color). Join us on the second Saturday of the month for Group Inquiry where we answer questions from the Recovery Dharma book. The third Saturday of the month is our speaker meeting

name: S.O.U.L. (Strength, Outreach, Unity, Liberation)
zoomLink: Hazelen Betty Ford, 283 W. Broadway - 1st Floor
notes: This meeting was created to provide a safe space for people who racially identify as Black, Indigenous, and/or People of Color. Any and all who identify as BIPOC (Black, Indigenous, and/or People of Color) are welcome at this meeting.

fellowship: Debtors Anonymous - African-American and African Diaspora
name: Black in DA
notes: This group is for members who identify as being of African Descent and working a program in Debtors Anonymous. We follow the principles of Debtors Anonymous. We are a 12 step group committed to people who want to stop incurring unsecured debt.

day: Saturday - Only 2nd Saturday of every month
name: Concept 5 Service Manual Study Meeting
notes: A 90min monthly Meeting on the 2nd Saturday of every month. We study the Al-Anon/Alateen Service Manual. Know your rights as a member! This meeting is a Person of Color focus. Great resource to get support and encouragement for our people of color trusted servants. All Are Welcome to attend and participate even if you don’t identify as a person of color. We allot time for Pressing Problems in Service, Service Celebrations, hear a person of color speaker on service and build a deeper understanding of the Al-Anon/Alateen service structure. // Zoom ID and password were updated as at 8 June 2024

name: La Paz Y Serenidad
notes: Meeting is in Spanish - 1 hour meeting - For more information visit www.cmainla.com and https://www.cmainla.com/meetings/la-paz-y-serenidad/?tsml-day=6

fellowship: Al-Anon (AFG) with AA participation (See notes) 4th Saturdays only
name: Young People of Color AFG
notes: As of 23 April 2024, this group meets on the 4th Saturday of the month at 11 AM EST.

fellowship: Sex and Love Addicts Anonymous (SLAA) and Sex Addicts Anonymous (SAA)
name: SLAA BIPOC Anorexia
notes: "As Black, Indigenous and People of Color, our sex and love addiction has often been impacted by our current experiences or historical legacies as targets of racism, discrimination and prejudice... Throughout history, racism has often included sexual domination as part of white supremacy. This includes the sexual abuse and sexual harassment of people of color by white people, as well as training people of color to think of themselves as animals, as someone else's property, as only good for work, and expendable...Many of us come to SLAA because we have a problem, and we find community. It is a wonderful relief to know that we are not alone with our sex and love addiction. However, for some people of color, it may be difficult to be in a community that is predominantly white." -From "People of Color in SLAA"

name: What's the T?
notes: WHAT'S THE T? is a midday check-in meeting designated for members of the CMA community who identify as Black, Indigenous People Of Color (BIPOC). This gathering provides a confidential, supportive space for BIPOC individuals to decompress and speak openly about recovery-related trauma in a safe and affirming environment.

name: SHE RECOVERS
notes:
This community connects 24/7 in the SHE RECOVERS Support for BLAIWOC Facebook group and gathers bi-weekly online on Saturdays from 11 AM PT – 12 PM PT.  The group is private, so one must request to join for safety and anonymity purposes. Also, the events are posted in the private group, so the dates of the meetings are available to group members or those who follow the public SHE RECOVERS organizational pages as they post schedules too. Created by the SHE RECOVERS Trusted Advisor for Healing Racism in Recovery, Shari Hampton, and facilitated by SHE RECOVERS Certified Professionals, this intra-community closed space and bi-weekly support group is designed for women of color to connect and heal.
This is a recovery group for all fellowships, and we define recovery more broadly than only from substance use. One can be recovering from any number of life situations, such as mental health issues, grief, loss, divorce, behavioral disorders like eating disorders, shopping, gambling, trauma including racism and oppression etc.

name: BIPOC of EA
notes:
"Click here, to access the BIPOC of EA meeting Google Doc.  Emotions Anonymous is for anyone who wants to become well emotionally.  BIPOC of EA (Emotions Anonymous) is a safe, sacred meeting space ONLY for people who identify as Black (African American), Indigenous (Native American), and People of Color.  People of Color refers to any non-White identity, including Latinx/Hispanic, Asian, Pacific Islander, Middle Eastern, etc.  Some may prefer to identify as members of the Global Majority.  In this sacred space, we can share without fear of retaliation or judgment.  
BIPOC of EA Racism and Recovery Questions - Google Docs  
If you do NOT identify as BIPOC or members of the Global Majority, the best way that you can support us is by not attending the meeting and honoring our sacred space.

name: BIPOC & Al-Anon Principles: Detaching From White Supremacy
notes: Al-Anon; all ages, abilities, genders, LGBITA, BIPOC - Meeting is 1.5 hours

name: Saturday Morning LGBTQ BIPOC Mtg
location: Hybrid. Meets in Oakland, CA and online

fellowship: Buddhist Recovery Network: 8 Step Recovery (All Recovery)

MOAD
notes: Meeting is for biologically born males of the African Diaspora who are descendants of the African continent who may live outside the continent irrespective of their citizenship and nationality. MOAD provides a safe space for men of African descent to share their experiences, strengths and hopes in an environment which fosters authenticity without retaliation.

Everyone is welcome to state what they will be working on, verbally or in the chat. 1 minute check-ins at the end of the hour are welcomed but optional. Stay as long or as little as you want 💕

name: Stoners of Color Open Share
notes: Stoners of color is a fiercely LGBTQ+ inclusive Marijuana Anonymous group specifically for people who identify as Black, Indigenous, or other People of color. This meeting is an open share meeting and lasts 60 minutes.

fellowship: Adult Children of Alcoholics/Dysfunctional Families (ACA) - Speaker Meeting
name: That One is Me!
notes:
Safety statement:
As people of the global majority, we acknowledge that the abuse and neglect we experienced as children is the result of direct experiences and trans-generational legacies of systemic racial subjugation and dehumanization. This ACA meeting is a safe space for PGM to acknowledge our pain, explore how we’ve internalized and unknowingly perpetuate racist beliefs, and celebrating our liberation from self limitation and deprivation.

name: Men of Color SLAA
notes: Open SLAA meetng for male identified & gender non comforming BIPOC folk - other fellowships welcome! (starting 2/27)

name: Bridge to Hope (International Chinese and English AA Meeting)
